titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 1, no. 44: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 44. (A.R. No. 735 of 1905.) GRĀMAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Śivayōganātha temple.

p.hi: M.K. Parakēsarivarman (Parāntaka I) Year 36: Kali 4044, 1477037 day, Makara, Sunday, star Rēvati = 943 A.D. 14th January

summary.p: This inscription is bilingual ie Sanskrit and Tamil.

The Sanskrit portion records that Veḷḷaṅkumara, a native of Puttūr, was bold and invincible in battles and he was the best of the Kēraḷites. He was a general of (Prince) Rājāditya and built a stone temple for the god Mahādēva at the village Mauligrāma, situated on the bank (of the river) Peṇṇai.

The Tamil portion repeats the some fact. It records that Veḷḷaṅkumaraṉ, a native of Nandikkaraiputtur in Maḷai-nāḍu was a chief servant of the Chōḷas and was a great general of Rājāditya, the son of Parāntaka alias Vīrasōḻa perumāṉaḍigaḷ. It further mentions that he consecrated (an image of) Āṟṟuttaḷi-perumāṉaḍigaḷ which was sixteen feet in height, inside the stone temple, with libation of water.
Published in A.S.I. Annual Report 1905-06, pp. 182-184.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . mauliḥ putturajanmākalī
balajayinā muttamaḥ keraḷānām
rājādittyasya sākṣā davicalitaca
mūnāyako mādhurān=teḥ mauligrāme
dhi peṇṇātaṭa dharaṇi śilāmandiram
mandarābham ma(ā)hādevam mahāśrīsthira
tara makaro datra veḷḷaṅkkumāraḥ .
nadu sthalīkratanirate śśivāspadam
śilāsthalī mabhirataye puradviṣaḥ
nijā mimā ndhiya miva supratiṣṭhatām
kumāra Ityakrata navāgrahārajaḥ .
viśvāśarmmavyāprati cārāt tritayan=tat
bhasmīRead tadbhasīta cakre daityapurām yena nimeṣā
t . mārārāti rddhāma kumāreda mayan=te bo
bhūṣītā dāyuśaRead ṭhadāyuṣam man=taḥ karaṇañca .
svastiśrī . kaliukavaruṣam nālāyirattu nāṟpa
ttu nālu matiraikoṉṭaRead matiraikoṇṭakopparakecaripaṉmakRead paṉmaṟku
ku yāṇṭu 3 10 6 Āvatu kaliuk koṭṭu nāḷ
patiṉāṉku nūṟāirattu Eḻupa yirattuRestored as eḻupatteḻāyirattu (see Ep. īnd. Vol. VIII P. 261.)
muppattu Eḻu Āka tirumuṭiyūr Āṟṟuttaḷi
perumāṉaṭikaḷ Uṭaiya m ti
rukkaṟṟaḷiyāka Amaippittu Ivvāṭṭai ma
kara nāyaṟṟuc caṉikkiḻamai peṟṟa Irevati nāṉ
ṟu kumbha ŌraiyālThis was wrongly read as kumbha dāraiyāl and corrected into kumbha dhāraiyāl
mūṉṟucci patiṉāṟu
Aṭiyil śrī Āṟṟut
taḷipperumāṉaṭikaḷai
t tirukkaṟṟaḷiyiṉ Uḷ
ṇayRead uḷḷeypuka Eḻuntaruḷu
vattuRead eḻuntaruḷivittu pradiṣṭaiRead pradiṣṭhai ceyvi
ttār coḻarkkaḷ mula
bhratyar śrīpirāntakadevarāṉa
śrīviracoḻap perumāṉaṭikaḷ
makaṉār rājādittadevar perum
paṭai nāyakar malaināṭṭu nan
tikkaraipputtūr veḷḷaṅ
kumaraṉ . Itu paṉmaāheśvara
rakṣai . rakṣippār śrīpātam talai
melaṉa .
Digital edition of SII 32, part 1, no. 44 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
